out_of_scope_note: Behavioral-economics theory paper (QJE 2002, Rabin) formalizing the 'law of small numbers' bias in an economic agent's beliefs about i.i.d. signal rates and financial-analyst talent distributions. The model is about a stylized economic decision-maker's inference, not a computational model of brain function, and there is no neural recording, no perceptual/sensory encoding claim, and no circuit-level mechanism. Per scope rules, social-science/economics modeling without neural data or a brain-computation model is out-of-scope, even though the gambler's-fallacy topic overlaps superficially with Bayesian-observer work in the lab.
